project_name: SeAH Wind Teesworks Monopile Manufacturing Facility
location_name: South Bank, Teesworks, Redcar and Cleveland
source_name: UK Government and Tees Valley Combined Authority
notes: Directly located in Tees Valley. Reported capital investment of £900m and 750 direct jobs. Annual production capacity reported as up to 200 monopiles.

project_name: JDR Cable Systems Cambois Subsea Cable Facility
delivery_risk_notes: Project remains under construction and completion should be reconfirmed.
source_name: JDR Cable Systems and North East Combined Authority
notes: Direct North East investment in a high-voltage subsea cable manufacturing plant. Reported to create 171 direct jobs. Completion and operating status should be updated when formally confirmed.
Direct Sunderland investment. Reported capital investment of £1bn and 1,000 jobs, with stated annual production capacity of 15.8 GWh.

project_type: Gas-fired power station with carbon capture
delivery_risk_notes: Reached financial close and construction is underway.
notes: Reached financial close in December 2024. Construction began from 2025 with operations targeted from 2028. Reported figures include more than 3,000 construction jobs and around 1,000 jobs supported during operations. The 4,000 figure is retained as the overall announced regional jobs headline and must not be added to the component categories. The approximately £4bn construction-contract figure covers NZT Power and the Northern Endurance Partnership jointly, so it is not recorded as standalone project capital investment.

project_name: Port of Blyth Battleship Wharf Expansion
location_name: Battleship Wharf, Port of Blyth, Northumberland
investor_name: Port of Blyth; Northumberland County Council; North East Combined Authority; The Crown Estate
delivery_risk_notes: Investment is announced but construction and delivery remain pending.
notes: Direct regional port infrastructure investment of up to £100m. Planned works include approximately three hectares of reclaimed land, up to 260m of quay extensions, channel and berth deepening, and heavy-duty handling equipment. The source refers to hundreds of potential jobs but does not state a precise verified figure, so jobs fields remain blank.

delivery_risk_notes: Delivery depends on infrastructure build-out and future tenant demand.
notes: Direct £150m regional investment in a 230-acre clean-energy development terminal. The source describes capacity to support offshore wind and other clean-energy projects. Up to 12,000 jobs is a wider jobs-supported ambition associated with the development and must not be interpreted as direct operational employment.

delivery_risk_notes: Potential investment remains at planning stage and is not committed.
notes: Direct Teesside hydrogen project at pre-application stage. The £2bn figure is potential investment rather than committed capital. Project sources describe more than 100 new operational jobs and an estimated £200m–£300m contribution to the local economy; this row records £200m as a conservative lower-bound economic impact estimate. Capacity reflects planned expansion to approximately 1,065 MW thermal.

location_name: Dogger Bank, North Sea; landfall and converter station in Redcar, Teesside
delivery_risk_notes: FID achieved; onshore and offshore construction are substantially advanced, with full operation targeted for 2026.
source_name: RWE and Sofia Offshore Wind Farm
notes: Approximately £3bn total project investment. The full project value is not attributed to the North East because major offshore and supply-chain expenditure occurs elsewhere. Independent research attributes £62.2m GVA and 490 FTE person-years to the North East. The 490 FTE person-years are retained in notes and are not treated as permanent operational jobs.

investor_name: Viridor; Tees Valley Energy Recovery Limited
delivery_risk_notes: Financial close reached and the EPC contractor has received notice to proceed.
source_name: Viridor and Tees Valley Energy Recovery Facility
notes: Financial close was reached in April 2026. The reported £2bn figure is the value of the 29-year waste-treatment contracts and is not recorded as capital expenditure. Construction employment is expected to peak at approximately 700 workers, with up to 50 permanent operational positions. The 750-job headline combines temporary and permanent roles occurring at different stages and must not be interpreted as simultaneous employment.

delivery_risk_notes: Phase one has signed a Low Carbon Hydrogen Agreement but remains planned rather than operational.
source_name: UK Government and EDF Renewables UK
notes: Phase one was selected under Hydrogen Allocation Round 1 and signed its Low Carbon Hydrogen Agreement in July 2025. The contracted initial capacity is 5.2 MW. No sufficiently reliable standalone capital or employment figure is currently recorded.

project_name: Dogger Bank Operations and Maintenance Base
project_type: Offshore wind operations and maintenance base
delivery_risk_notes: The base was completed and officially opened in 2023 and now supports long-term wind-farm operations.
notes: Direct North East operations base at the Port of Tyne. Official project announcements describe more than 200 direct regional jobs. The completed facility contains office, training and welfare infrastructure for around 400 employees who may be based at the facility or offshore; 400 is not recorded as direct operational employment to avoid overstating the verified jobs figure.

project_name: ORE Catapult Blyth Turbine Testing Expansion
project_type: Offshore wind research and testing facility
location_name: National Renewable Energy Centre, Blyth, Northumberland
delivery_risk_notes: UKRI capital funding has been awarded and the new blade hall and drivetrain upgrade are targeted for commissioning by 2028.
notes: £85.6m capital investment to expand ORE Catapult's Blyth testing facilities. The project includes blade testing up to 150 metres and drivetrain testing up to 23 MW, with future scalability to 180 metres and 28 MW. Official sources report 30 new skilled jobs and support for five PhD researchers per year. PhD positions are not added to the direct operational-jobs total.

project_name: Osbit Port of Blyth Assembly and Service Facility
project_type: Offshore wind equipment assembly and testing facility
location_name: Bates Clean Energy Terminal, Port of Blyth, Northumberland
investor_name: Osbit Ltd; Venterra Group; Port of Blyth
delivery_risk_notes: The facility is fully operational and has already delivered offshore-wind installation and testing equipment.
notes: Fully operational 3,350 square metre assembly and service facility at Wimbourne Quay within the Bates Clean Energy Terminal. Official reporting states that the development created 33 skilled regional jobs and doubled permanent positions at Osbit's assembly facility. The project received Business Growth Fund grant support, but no reliable total investment value is disclosed, so capital fields remain blank.

project_name: Inch Cape Offshore Substation Wallsend Fabrication
location_name: Smulders yard, Wallsend, Newcastle upon Tyne
investor_name: Inch Cape Offshore Wind Limited; ESB; Red Rock Renewables
delivery_risk_notes: The offshore transformer module and jacket were assembled in Wallsend and successfully installed offshore in 2025.
source_name: Inch Cape Offshore Wind and North East Offshore Wind Strategy
notes: A team of more than 250 local employees fitted out and assembled the Siemens Energy offshore transformer module and its 68-metre jacket foundation at Smulders' Wallsend yard over approximately 18 months. Around 80 local UK subcontractors supported the work. The 250 figure is recorded as project-stage employment rather than permanent operational employment. No standalone capital value for the North East fabrication package is publicly disclosed.
